title: Ex-Offender Halfway Houses - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Ex-Offender Halfway Houses 10 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Women Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLACKAMAS COUNTY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 707 Main Street Suite 220 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 560-0624 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE SPECIAL PROGRAMS OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 229 NE 181st Avenue Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 465-2749 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing, treatment, and supportive services for individuals recovering from addiction. Programs are led by peer mentors. Intensive outpatient (IOP) and outpatient substance use disorder treatment are available at multiple locations. 1049 SW Baseline Street Suite D480 Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (971) 386-3443 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN HOUSING AND DEVELOPMENT SUPPORTIVE HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of affordable housing options, including transitional housing and permanent supportive housing. These options combine affordable housing with essential services such as health care, recovery support, employment assistance, and veteran services. Also provides subsidized rental units to help ensure long-term housing stability 523 NW Everett Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness, individuals in recovery, those struggling with mental health challenges, living with HIV/AIDS or other disabling conditions, and/or a history of criminal involvement, and veterans experiencing homelessness Hours: Varies by property Email (503) 548-4931 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL CITY CONCERN IMANI CENTER HOUSING RAPID RESPONSE INFORMATIONAL RECORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Responds to the needs of houseless ex-offenders through safe housing programs, medical and mental care, addiction treatment, and other vital supports. 2020 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Houseless individuals age 18 and over Must be referred by parole officer or other re-entry program Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 226-4060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FREE ON THE OUTSIDE FREE ON THE OUTSIDE HOUSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transitional housing and sober living facilities. Clients may not use illegal drugs or alcohol during their stay. 529 Molalla Avenue Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Anyone experiencing housing instability. Does not turn people away for their criminal history or credit. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 383-1834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC ROOSEVELT CROSSING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and supportive services to individuals released in Lane County from prison or jail, and currently on correctional supervision in Lane County. Average length of program is 60-90 days. Services in addition to housing include: case management; behavioral health; drug and alcohol treatment; as well as assistance with clothing, ID, bus passes/bicycles, employment search, and finding long-term housing. Specialized services available for veterans. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Male-identifying individuals on correctional supervision in or releasing to Lane County. -Priority for transitional housing provided to those releasing from state prison who would otherwise be homeless and who are at greatest risk to become reengaged with the criminal legal system without supports. -Participants expected to follow program rules, remain clean and sober (tested by urinalysis and breathalyzer), obtain full-time employment or be enrolled in school full-time, and follow all conditions of their supervision. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 485-8341 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SPONSORS INC DOWNTOWN CAMPUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing and supportive services for individuals released in Lane County from Oregon State Prison or the local jail who are currently on correctional supervision. The average length of the program is 90 to 120 days. Services include case management, behavioral health support, drug and alcohol treatment, and assistance with clothing, identification, bus passes or bicycles, employment search, and finding long-term housing. 338 Highway 99 N Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female, released from Oregon State Prison or Lane County Jail, and under supervision in Lane County. Participants must follow program rules, remain clean and sober (verified by urinalysis and breathalyzer testing), obtain full-time employment or enroll in school full-time, and comply with all conditions of their supervision. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 485-8341 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSCENDING HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a structured housing environment for those seeking recovery from addiction, alcoholism, incarceration, and/or mental health issues. 29 locations, including Aid & Assist Community Restoration, and culturally responsive Latino and Latina housing. 347A 1st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Must be working towards abstinence Hours: Not applicable Email (971) 449-6793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM RESTORATION HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides transitional housing services. Guests will have access to case management, job readiness training and placement assistance, life skills classes, relapse prevention, mental health supports, and faith-based guidance. Individuals who have completed the program may be referred to long term rehousing re-entry services. 650 Locust Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Men age 18 and older with any criminal history Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 363-2805 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WELCOME HOME OREGON INC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides re-entry housing along with assistance for food, clothing, employment, and mentorship. 510 NW 4th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Individuals who were recently released from a correctional facility and are on supervised release Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment only Email (541) 474-5178 Get Directions More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLACKAMAS COUNTY OFFICE BRIDGES TO CHANGE SPECIAL PROGRAMS OFFICE BRIDGES TO CHANGE WASHINGTON COUNTY OFFICE CENTRAL CITY CONCERN SUPPORTIVE HOUSING CENTRAL CITY CONCERN HOUSING RAPID RESPONSE INFORMATIONAL RECORD FREE ON THE OUTSIDE FREE ON THE OUTSIDE HOUSING SPONSORS INC ROOSEVELT CROSSING TRANSCENDING HOPE TRANSCENDING HOPE UNION GOSPEL MISSION OF SALEM RESTORATION HOUSE WELCOME HOME OREGON INC WELCOME HOME OREGON INC
